title: cotylelobium scabriusculum
intro: Cotylelobium scabriusculum, commonly known as the 'Mountain Ironwood' or 'Kapok Tree', is a species of flowering plant in the family Dipterocarpaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The therapeutic actions of Cotylelobium scabriusculum are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The extracts of Cotylelobium scabriusculum have been studied for their potential therapeutic applications in the treatment of various diseases, including cancer, diabetes, and cardiovascular disorders.
benefits: Cotylelobium scabriusculum has many benefits, such as its use in traditional medicine to alleviate various health issues, including fever reduction, pain relief, and the treatment of respiratory problems like bronchitis and asthma. The plant's extracts have also been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections and promote wound healing. Furthermore, Cotylelobium scabriusculum is rich in antioxidants, which can protect against oxidative stress and potentially reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and heart disease. By harnessing the benefits of this plant, individuals can experience improved overall health, increased energy levels, and a better quality of life.
actions: Cotylelobium scabriusculum has many therapeutic actions, such as exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reducing joint pain and swelling, allowing individuals to engage in daily activities like gardening and hiking without discomfort. Its antimicrobial properties also make it effective in wound healing, preventing infections, and promoting the growth of new tissue, thereby enabling people to recover quickly from injuries and surgeries, and resume their normal routines. Additionally, Cotylelobium scabriusculum has been found to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, which help protect cells from damage and reduce the risk of certain types of cancer, giving people peace of mind and a sense of security. By reducing stress and anxiety through its adaptogenic properties, Cotylelobium scabriusculum also helps individuals manage their mental well-being, enabling them to handle the demands of daily life with greater ease and confidence.
constituents: Cotylelobium scabriusculum has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, alkaloids, saponins, and triterpenoids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. These constituents, including kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin, have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by reducing cholesterol levels and preventing the formation of blood clots. Additionally, the plant's saponins and triterpenoids, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, have been found to have anti-cancer and immunomodulatory effects, helping to protect against various diseases and improve overall well-being. By harnessing the medicinal properties of Cotylelobium scabriusculum, people can benefit from its potential to reduce the risk of chronic diseases, improve mental clarity, and enhance their overall quality of life.
parts: Cotylelobium scabriusculum has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, roots, and bark, which are traditionally used to treat various ailments. The leaves of the plant are said to contain medicinal constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant are known to contain compounds like alkaloids and glycosides, which may have potential anti-diarrheal and anti-bacterial effects. The bark of the plant is reported to contain tannins and saponins, which are thought to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, respectively.
side_effects: Cotylelobium scabriusculum can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant can also lead to kidney damage and liver toxicity, resulting in symptoms like fatigue, weakness, and yellowing of the skin and eyes. In some cases, improper use of Cotylelobium scabriusculum may cause allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Additionally, improper preparation or dosage of the plant can also lead to cardiovascular issues, including rapid heartbeat, high blood pressure, and cardiac arrhythmia.
